answer:
Yes, private applications used by sellers or vendors to access their own business data remain free and exempt from Selling Partner API (SP-API) fees [1][2].

For third-party developers, Amazon implemented an annual fee of $1,400 effective January 31, 2026 [1][2]. While a tiered structure for GET call volume was introduced, per-call overage fees have been postponed indefinitely following developer feedback [2]. All developers, regardless of subscription status, retain free access to core resources such as documentation, software development kits (SDKs), and sandbox testing environments [1].

results.title: Amazon introduces fees for third-party developer API access in 2026
